title: Korean Corn Cheese

ingredients.name: sweet corn kernels (canned or freshly boiled)

subs.name: frozen peas and corn mix for extra colour and texture
50 g grated Amul processed cheese + 50 g mozzarella for better melt — processed cheese melts smoother in Indian kitchen conditions
finely diced onion or spring onion for gentle sweetness

steps.text: If using canned corn, drain it well and pat dry with kitchen paper — excess moisture makes the dish watery. If using fresh cobs, strip the kernels and boil with a pinch of salt for 3 minutes, then drain thoroughly.

Melt butter in a frying pan or small kadai over medium flame. Add the corn and capsicum and toss together. Cook for 3–4 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the corn starts to colour at the edges and smells sweet and nutty.
Season with sugar, salt and black pepper. Stir to coat all the kernels evenly. Flatten the corn mixture into an even layer across the pan.

Scatter the shredded mozzarella generously over the top in a thick, even layer. Cover the pan with a lid and cook on the lowest possible flame for 4–5 minutes until the cheese has melted completely and formed golden, bubbly patches.
Scatter chopped spring onion greens over the top and serve immediately in the pan while the cheese is still stretchy. Leftovers reheat well under a grill or in an oven.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: cheese → 50 g grated Amul processed cheese + 50 g mozzarella for better melt — processed cheese melts smoother in Indian kitchen conditions; capsicum → finely diced onion or spring onion for gentle sweetness; corn → frozen peas and corn mix for extra colour and texture
indian_kitchen_adaptation: Corn cheese is completely at home in an Indian kitchen. Sweet corn is sold year-round at Indian vegetable markets and as canned or frozen kernels in supermarkets. Pre-shredded mozzarella (sold as 'pizza cheese' in Indian supermarkets) melts well on a stovetop; a tawa or frying pan with a lid works in place of an oven. A pinch of Kashmiri chilli flakes over the melted cheese adds an Indian flourish.
